SEARCH
Package server.control

Interface DomainController

All Known Implementing Classes:
AuthController, NotificationController, ParkController, ReportController, ReservationController, VisitController
public interface DomainController
A handler for one domain's slice of the server's request space (orders, reservations, parks, …). RequestRouter owns one instance of each controller and dispatches every incoming ClientRequest to the controller that claims its RequestType.

Threading: a single instance of each controller is shared across all client-handling threads (one router, built once in OrderServer). Implementations MUST therefore be stateless — hold only immutable collaborators such as DAOs as fields, never per-request mutable state, or two concurrent clients will corrupt each other.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    ServerResponse
    handle(ClientRequest request, ClientSession session)
    Handles one request and produces the response to send back.
    Set<RequestType>
    handledTypes()
    The set of operation types this controller is responsible for.

  • Method Details

    • handledTypes

      Set<RequestType> handledTypes()
      The set of operation types this controller is responsible for. The router builds its dispatch map from these sets at construction time; if two controllers return overlapping sets the router fails fast.
      Returns:
      the request types handled by this controller; should be a stable, immutable set (the router treats it as fixed)

    • handle

      ServerResponse handle(ClientRequest request, ClientSession session)
      Handles one request and produces the response to send back. Called concurrently from multiple client threads, so this method must rely only on its arguments and immutable fields (see the threading note on the interface). The correlation id is stamped centrally by OrderServer before the response is sent, so implementations must NOT set it here.
      Parameters:
      request - the incoming request; its RequestType is guaranteed to be one of this controller's handledTypes()
      session - the originating client's session (for subscription-aware ops)
      Returns:
      the response to return to the client; never null
